title: Code Review: Add user search with task counts

segments.narration: This PR adds a new user search endpoint for a dashboard widget. The change looks simple: just wiring up a new route.
Here's the new search endpoint. It accepts a query parameter and validates it. So far, nothing alarming.
But wait. The security auditor flagged this immediately: there's no authentication check. Anyone can call this endpoint.
Here's the first critical issue: a full table scan. We fetch ALL users from the database, then filter in JavaScript.
This query has no WHERE clause. With ten thousand users, we're transferring megabytes of data just to search for one name.
Now comes the N plus one query anti-pattern. For each matching user, we make a separate database call to count their tasks.
If fifty users match the search, that's fifty separate queries. The database connection pool will be exhausted under load.
The junior reviewer asked why we stringify then immediately parse the JSON. There's no reason: it just adds overhead.
Finally, the error handling is generic. When this times out at three AM, on-call engineers will have zero debugging information.
The council voted to block this PR. Three critical issues: no authentication, full table scan, and N plus one queries.
